text: The surgeon uses a barb suture or V-lock to secure the cystic duct stump, as clipping or endo-looping was deemed inadvisable for this particular case.
The surgeon takes an anchoring stitch at the proximal end of the cystic duct stump and begins placing running sutures using a barb suture technique.
The surgeon takes a small interval bite of approximately two millimeters on the cystic duct stump, ensuring each suture bite is snug.
The surgeon has divided the neck and the cystic duct junction, separating these anatomical structures.
The surgeon uses scissors to divide the remaining tissue while employing an interlock stitch to secure the area, retracting the tissue properly with a mega suture card.
